target.name: Hermeus
target.founder: AJ Piplica, Skyler Shuford, Glenn Case, Mike Smayda

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Supersonic & Hypersonic (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 1 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Supersonic & Hypersonic, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Hermeus, a Series C-stage developer of supersonic and hypersonic aircraft technology, was last valued at $1.0B post-money following $350M in total capital raised. The supersonic and hypersonic sector comprises a sparse comparable universe: two public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and one identifiable private peer (Boom Supersonic), limiting traditional peer-based triangulation.

Stage-progression methodology is the applicable valuation approach here. Using the $1.0B Series C post-money valuation as the base and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for progression to the next institutional funding stage yields an implied valuation of $1.7B. This method reflects observed funding dynamics within early-stage aerospace and defense technology companies advancing from Series C.

Peer-median comparison was not feasible due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow Supersonic & Hypersonic sector. Broadening to all-sector peers would introduce unacceptable category risk and was therefore excluded from this analysis.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.4B to $2.0B, centered at $1.7B. This relatively compressed range reflects reliance on a single methodology rather than cross-validation across multiple approaches. Key uncertainties include regulatory timelines for certification, capital requirements for prototype development, and customer commitments, all of which remain critical to downside/upside scenarios not quantified in this data set.
